danger_signals.visual_form: Large hostile creatures in mountain pass
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate physical threat; avalanche hazard indicates environmental danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Cursed spirits in Vale of Shadows crypts
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural threat; necromantic corruption of sacred spaces
Organized hostile force; settlement under active assault

danger_signals.visual_form: Harsh environmental conditions in Icewind Dale
danger_signals.meaning: Magical corruption of world-state; supernatural interference
Loss of trust in perceived allies; deception threat
Environmental life-support compromised; settlement survival threatened

safety_signals.meaning: Civilized safe zone; quest origin point

safety_signals.visual_form: Archdruid in village; magical authority figure
safety_signals.meaning: Protective force; druidic magic maintains village stability

safety_signals.visual_form: Assembled adventuring group of up to 6 characters
safety_signals.meaning: Strength in numbers; capability to face threats

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kidnapped/corrupted villagers held by lizard men
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Village settlement; Vale of Shadows crypts; Dragon's Eye caverns
Fortified military outpost; Larrel's command base
Dragon's Eye caverns; scrying through stolen artifact
Multi-location plot spanning Easthaven, Kuldahar, fortress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Abnormal weather patterns visible; reduced magical warmth from guardian tree; missing villagers absent from settlements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs report kidnappings and environmental collapse; Hrothgar organizes defensive expedition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Undead creatures manifest in Vale of Shadows; cursed barbarian spirit encountered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kresselack directs party away from immediate threat, indicating deeper source

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heartstone Gem in use by Marilith; lizard men army holding captives in caverns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Organized enemy forces; coordinated kidnapping operations; Yxunomei using scrying to maintain control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Orog forces attacking Kuldahar; leadership structure compromised by shapeshifter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arundel appears wounded/dying; village defenses overwhelmed; party leadership questioned

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larrel at Severed Hand appears insane; fortress isolated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest required to restore sanity to key NPC before critical information available

environmental_storytelling.example: Party travels from tavern (safety) → mountain pass (danger) → village → crypts → caverns → fortress, each location revealing plot layers
Giant tree losing magical warmth signals supernatural threat; abnormal weather demonstrates environmental world-state collapse
Severed Hand fortress and Kuldahar's giant tree represent settlement power structures; their compromise indicates threat severity
Vale of Shadows crypts, Dragon's Eye caverns establish faction control zones; presence of specific enemy types defines territorial ownership
Arundel's death, Larrel's insanity, shapeshifter assassination attempt communicate escalating crisis through leadership destruction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively organizes expedition; invites party to Tavern; represents civilian leadership response to crisis
Provides quests; instructs party on threat investigation; attempts to defend village; dies warning party of deeper threat
Morally wounds true Arundel; taunts party; represents enemy deception capability
Provides cryptic direction away from Vale of Shadows; indicates deeper threat existence; constrained by curse
Uses scrying via Heartstone Gem; commands lizard men forces; orchestrates kidnappings; represents principal antagonistic force
Unable to function until sanity restored; requires party intervention; becomes critical to discovering final threat source
Execute organized siege on Kuldahar; indicate coordinated command structure; represent visible manifestation of enemy military power

notes: Icewind Dale communicates world-state through environmental degradation (magical warmth loss, weather anomalies), NPC state collapse (leadership assassination, sanity loss), and progressive enemy revelation (undead → demonic → military siege). Danger manifests through creature encounters that escalate in sophistication: minor undead → powerful demon → organized military forces → infiltration threat. The game uses geographical progression and architectural authority (sacred tree, fortress stronghold) as visual anchors for safety/danger zones. Political situation emerges through NPC reliability shifts and leadership structure compromise, culminating in shapeshifter assassination indicating pervasive infiltration threat.
